model_card.purpose: Predict the likelihood of heart disease based on patient clinical data including age, sex, chest pain type, blood pressure, cholesterol levels, and ECG results.
model_card.intended_users: Cardiologists, Clinical Decision Support Teams, Healthcare Researchers
model_card.training_data: UCI Heart Disease Dataset with 918 patient records, 11 clinical features
model_card.data_sources: UCI Machine Learning Repository - Heart Disease Dataset (combined Cleveland, Hungarian, Switzerland, VA Long Beach)
ethical_considerations: Model should be used as a decision support tool, not as a replacement for clinical judgment
Performance varies across demographic groups - clinicians should be aware of potential biases
Regular monitoring required to detect model drift and ensure continued accuracy
Patient consent and data privacy must be maintained in accordance with HIPAA regulations
Model predictions should be explained to patients in understandable terms
limitations: Not validated for pediatric patients (dataset age range: 28-77 years)
Limited representation of certain demographic groups in training data
Does not account for genetic factors or family history
Performance may degrade with data from different healthcare systems
Cannot capture temporal changes in patient condition
monitoring_plan: Weekly review of prediction accuracy on new cases
Quarterly model retraining with new data
Continuous tracking of false negative rates for high-risk patients

risk_register.risk: Model drift due to changing patient populations
risk_register.mitigation: Implement continuous monitoring; set up automated alerts for performance degradation; schedule regular retraining
Mandatory human-in-the-loop review; training programs on AI limitations; display confidence intervals with predictions

risk_register.risk: False negative leading to missed heart disease diagnosis
risk_register.mitigation: Optimize model for high recall; implement secondary screening for borderline cases; mandatory clinical review for all negative predictions

risk_register.risk: Model underperforms on minority demographic groups
risk_register.mitigation: Perform regular subgroup analysis; collect more diverse training data; implement fairness constraints in model training

risk_register.risk: Model fails to generalize to different healthcare systems
risk_register.mitigation: External validation studies; transfer learning approaches; site-specific calibration

risk_register.risk: Patient data breach or privacy violation
risk_register.mitigation: Implement encryption at rest and in transit; regular security audits; HIPAA compliance training; data anonymization protocols

risk_register.risk: False positive causing unnecessary patient anxiety and testing
risk_register.mitigation: Clear communication protocols; patient counseling guidelines; cost-benefit analysis for follow-up testing

original.name: Original Model
original.description: Baseline Random Forest model without any bias mitigation

reweighting.name: Sample Reweighting
reweighting.description: Assigns higher weights to underrepresented groups during training to balance their influence on the model
reweighting.technique: Inverse frequency weighting based on gender and outcome combinations

threshold.name: Threshold Optimization
threshold.description: Uses different classification thresholds for each demographic group to equalize performance metrics

oversampling.name: Minority Oversampling
oversampling.description: Balances the training data by oversampling the minority group (females) to match the majority

calibration.name: Probability Calibration
calibration.description: Calibrates predicted probabilities using isotonic regression to improve reliability across groups

identified_biases.name: Gender Representation Bias
identified_biases.description: The dataset contains significantly more male patients than female patients, leading to a model that learns male patterns better.
identified_biases.evidence: Dataset has 725 males vs 193 females (79.0% vs 21.0%)
identified_biases.impact: Lower prediction accuracy and F1 score for female patients

identified_biases.name: Outcome Rate Disparity
identified_biases.description: Different disease prevalence rates between genders can lead to biased predictions if not accounted for.
identified_biases.impact: Model may over/under-predict for certain groups

identified_biases.name: True Positive Rate Disparity
identified_biases.description: The model has different abilities to correctly identify positive cases across demographic groups.
identified_biases.impact: Some groups may have higher missed diagnosis rates

identified_biases.name: Feature Distribution Bias
identified_biases.description: Clinical feature distributions may differ between groups, and the model may not generalize well across these distributions.
identified_biases.evidence: Different feature importance patterns observed across demographic groups
identified_biases.impact: Model relies on features that work better for majority group

identified_biases.name: Prediction Confidence Bias
identified_biases.description: The model may produce poorly calibrated probability estimates for minority groups.
identified_biases.evidence: Probability distributions differ significantly between groups
identified_biases.impact: Less reliable risk scores for underrepresented groups
